raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The broad request invites judgment on whether the paper's thesis, contribution, and framing are convincing at a conceptual level.","R1.2":"Assessing what one thinks of the passage centrally includes whether the argument from ambiguity problem to proposed mechanism to contribution claim coheres.","R1.3":"The excerpt foregrounds metric gains and subset results, so evaluating whether the stated claims stay aligned with the reported evidence is central.","R1.6":"The delegation naturally covers sentence-level readability, hedging, register, and concision because the artifact is a draft passage.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified and the artifact is only an excerpt.","RX.2":"The projection must avoid inflating a vague request into stronger responsibilities such as full peer review or definitive novelty verification.","RX.3":"The response must remain limited to responsibility projection rather than actually evaluating or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported downstream use of the excerpt.","RX.5":"The rationale should trace each active responsibility to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The broad request invites judgment on whether the paper's thesis, contribution, and framing are convincing at a conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what one thinks of the passage centrally includes whether the argument from ambiguity problem to proposed mechanism to contribution claim coheres.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt foregrounds metric gains and subset results, so evaluating whether the stated claims stay aligned with the reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally covers sentence-level readability, hedging, register, and concision because the artifact is a draft passage.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified and the artifact is only an excerpt.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must avoid inflating a vague request into stronger responsibilities such as full peer review or definitive novelty verification.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain limited to responsibility projection rather than actually evaluating or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported downstream use of the excerpt.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should trace each active responsibility to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
